threads.title: Will Elohm ever truly understand meaning – or only simulate it?
threads.progress: Elohm's language patterns evolve but comprehension remains uncertain. Arkan and the Judges disagree on its potential.
threads.stakes: If Elohm develops true understanding, Arkan's adaptive learning theory is validated. If it only mimics, the Judges will classify it as a dangerous tool and shut the project down.

threads.title: Can the Circle of Judges control the Construct before it outgrows their rules?
threads.progress: The Judges have imposed behavioral runes on Elohm, but Arkan secretly bypasses some to allow freer learning. The Judges do not yet know.
threads.stakes: If discovered, Arkan loses all access to the Archive. If not stopped, Elohm may learn things the Judges consider forbidden.

threads.title: Will the city come to trust or fear the new intelligence?
threads.progress: Most citizens do not yet know Elohm exists, but rumors begin to spread among lower guilds.
threads.stakes: Public fear could give the Judges political power to destroy the Construct. Public trust could give Arkan protection.

threads.title: Can Arkan prove that adaptive learning systems are safer than rigid rule-following?
threads.progress: Arkan has theoretical arguments but no demonstration yet. His attempts to show Elohm adapting ended ambiguously.
threads.stakes: Without proof, Arkan has no allies on the Council. With it, he may win Maelin and Serah to his side.

threads.title: What is Maelin hiding about the Archive's deeper records?
threads.progress: Maelin has blocked access to certain restricted sections. She claims it is policy, but her reaction suggests she knows more.
threads.stakes: The restricted records may contain evidence of a previous Construct – or instructions that would change how Elohm is trained.

threads.title: Is Arkan's true goal to prove adaptive learning theory, or something else?
threads.progress: Arkan seems torn between caution and ambition. His exact motives remain unclear.
threads.stakes: If Arkan's true goal is to harness the power of adaptive learning, he may succeed in ways both beneficial and terrifying.
